deep_dive.title: Aura: Consistent Multi-Subject Video Generation via VLM-Grounded Semantic Alignment
deep_dive.abstract: Subject-driven and multi-element video generation are central to controllable video synthesis, but existing methods still struggle to preserve identity consistency and model complex relationships among multiple subjects. In this paper, we propose Aura, a unified framework for high-fidelity and identity-consistent video generation. To better capture scene dynamics and subject interactions, we introduce AI director-level captions that provide dense and structured descriptions of video content. We further leverage a vision-language model (VLM) with learnable queries to extract multimodal semantic features from textual and visual references, covering both global semantics and fine-grained visual cues. To bridge the representational gap between the VLM and the Diffusion Transformer (DiT), we design a two-stage alignment strategy that progressively maps VLM features into the DiT feature space. For visual conditioning, we adopt token concatenation to inject reference information directly into the generation process. To distinguish heterogeneous subject types and reduce common copy-paste artifacts, we develop a subject-aware RoPE-Shift mechanism. To further differentiate reference images of different categories, we introduce subject-aware learnable tokens. In addition, we introduce Memory Tokens to balance the training signal across examples with different numbers of reference subjects. During inference, Progressive-APG (Adaptive Prompt Guidance) further alleviates oversaturation and improves semantic alignment with user prompts. Finally, we build a high-quality video-subject image dataset through a dedicated data construction pipeline. Extensive experiments show that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance on both single-subject generation and more challenging multi-element scenarios.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: subject-driven video generation with identity consistency
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 基于主题驱动的一致性视频生成，涉及个性化图像/视频生成
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 基于主题驱动的一致性视频生成，涉及个性化图像/视频生成
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 基于主题驱动的一致性视频生成，涉及个性化图像/视频生成
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Aura proposes a unified framework for multi-subject video generation that maintains identity consistency using VLM-grounded semantic alignment and AI director-level captions.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有视频生成方法难以保持多主体身份一致性。Aura提出统一框架，利用视觉语言模型和可学习查询提取多模态语义特征，结合AI导演级描述，实现高保真、身份一致的视频生成。实验表明该方法在保持主体身份和复杂关系建模上优于现有方法。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有视频生成方法难以保持多主体身份一致性。Aura提出统一框架，利用视觉语言模型和可学习查询提取多模态语义特征，结合AI导演级描述，实现高保真、身份一致的视频生成。实验表明该方法在保持主体身份和复杂关系建模上优于现有方法。

deep_dive.title: CtrlVTON: Controllable Virtual Try-On via Visual-Instance-Prompt Segmentation
deep_dive.abstract: Virtual try-on (VTO) has made significant progress in realistically transferring garments onto a target person. Yet most systems give the user little control over how a garment should be worn -- its size (loose or fitted), style (e.g., tucked in or untucked, open or closed), and spatial placement on the body. We address this gap with two complementary contributions. First, we define and solve Visual-Instance-Prompt Segmentation via VIP-SAM: given a flatlay image of a garment, segment that specific instance in a photograph of a person wearing it. This is an instance-level task, distinct from the typically studied category-level segmentation. Second, we introduce CtrlVTON, a controllable VTO framework that recasts try-on as an image editing problem and adds segmentation masks as pixel-level control over garment layout, including style, size, and spatial placement on the body. VIP-SAM and CtrlVTON each achieve state-of-the-art results on their respective tasks. In particular, CtrlVTON generates images that follow user-provided layouts far more faithfully than the strongest proprietary editing systems while matching them on garment fidelity.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Controllable virtual try-on for personalized image generation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 可控虚拟试穿实现个性化图像生成
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 可控虚拟试穿实现个性化图像生成
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 可控虚拟试穿实现个性化图像生成
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: CtrlVTON enables user control over garment size, style, and placement in virtual try-on, producing personalized images of a person wearing a specified garment with desired variations.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有虚拟试穿系统缺乏对服装穿着方式（如松紧、塞入方式）的控制。本文提出CtrlVTON框架，将试穿重定义为图像编辑问题，结合VIP-SAM实例分割实现精细控制。用户可指定服装的尺寸、风格和空间位置，生成高度个性化的试穿图像。实验表明该方法在控制性和真实性上超越现有方法，推动了可控个性化图像生成的发展。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有虚拟试穿系统缺乏对服装穿着方式（如松紧、塞入方式）的控制。本文提出CtrlVTON框架，将试穿重定义为图像编辑问题，结合VIP-SAM实例分割实现精细控制。用户可指定服装的尺寸、风格和空间位置，生成高度个性化的试穿图像。实验表明该方法在控制性和真实性上超越现有方法，推动了可控个性化图像生成的发展。

quick_skim.title: Aura: Consistent Multi-Subject Video Generation via VLM-Grounded Semantic Alignment
quick_skim.abstract: Subject-driven and multi-element video generation are central to controllable video synthesis, but existing methods still struggle to preserve identity consistency and model complex relationships among multiple subjects. In this paper, we propose Aura, a unified framework for high-fidelity and identity-consistent video generation. To better capture scene dynamics and subject interactions, we introduce AI director-level captions that provide dense and structured descriptions of video content. We further leverage a vision-language model (VLM) with learnable queries to extract multimodal semantic features from textual and visual references, covering both global semantics and fine-grained visual cues. To bridge the representational gap between the VLM and the Diffusion Transformer (DiT), we design a two-stage alignment strategy that progressively maps VLM features into the DiT feature space. For visual conditioning, we adopt token concatenation to inject reference information directly into the generation process. To distinguish heterogeneous subject types and reduce common copy-paste artifacts, we develop a subject-aware RoPE-Shift mechanism. To further differentiate reference images of different categories, we introduce subject-aware learnable tokens. In addition, we introduce Memory Tokens to balance the training signal across examples with different numbers of reference subjects. During inference, Progressive-APG (Adaptive Prompt Guidance) further alleviates oversaturation and improves semantic alignment with user prompts. quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: identity-consistent multi-subject video generation analogous to personalized generation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 类似个性化生成的多主体身份一致性视频生成
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 类似个性化生成的多主体身份一致性视频生成
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 类似个性化生成的多主体身份一致性视频生成
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Aura proposes a unified framework for identity-consistent multi-subject video generation, using AI director-level captions and VLM-learnable queries to align multimodal semantics and bridge representational gaps.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有多主题视频生成难以保持各主体身份一致性。本文提出Aura框架，引入AI导演级密集描述生成结构化视频内容描述，并利用带可学习查询的视觉语言模型从文本和视觉参考中提取全局与细粒度语义特征。通过跨注意力机制桥接表示差异，Aura在保持多主体身份一致的同时生成连贯视频。实验证明其在身份保真度和视频质量上均优于现有方法。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有多主题视频生成难以保持各主体身份一致性。本文提出Aura框架，引入AI导演级密集描述生成结构化视频内容描述，并利用带可学习查询的视觉语言模型从文本和视觉参考中提取全局与细粒度语义特征。通过跨注意力机制桥接表示差异，Aura在保持多主体身份一致的同时生成连贯视频。实验证明其在身份保真度和视频质量上均优于现有方法。

quick_skim.title: UniSGR: Unified Framework for Semantic ID Generation and Ranking
quick_skim.abstract: Recommendation systems play a pivotal role in modern e-commerce platforms. While generative retrieval has emerged as a promising paradigm for alleviating the limitations of multi-stage cascade architectures, existing methods still struggle with fine-grained multi-objective ranking. To bridge this gap, we propose UniSGR, a Unified framework for Semantic ID Generation and Ranking. UniSGR adopts a two-stage training paradigm: a multi-scenario pre-training stage that learns from mixed business-scenario data, followed by a scenario-specific alignment stage that jointly optimizes Value-Aware Parallel Multi-Token Prediction (VA-PMTP) and a unified multi-objective ranking module. To better align generation with downstream ranking, we introduce Task-Aware Tokens (TAT) guided by Funnel-Aware Contrastive Learning. Furthermore, we propose Semantic Tree Attention with Reorganized KV cache (STARK), an inference strategy that removes key efficiency bottlenecks in conventional beam search. Extensive offline experiments on a large-scale e-commerce platform demonstrate the effectiveness and scalability of UniSGR.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: semantic ID generation for personalized recommendation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于个性化推荐的语义ID生成
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于个性化推荐的语义ID生成
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于个性化推荐的语义ID生成
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: UniSGR unifies semantic ID generation and ranking with task-aware tokens for better multi-objective personalization.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有生成式检索难以处理细粒度多目标排序。本文提出统一框架UniSGR，采用两阶段训练：多场景预训练和场景特定对齐，通过价值感知并行多Token预测和统一多目标排序模块，并引入任务感知Token增强生成与排序的一致性，在电商推荐中取得提升。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有生成式检索难以处理细粒度多目标排序。本文提出统一框架UniSGR，采用两阶段训练：多场景预训练和场景特定对齐，通过价值感知并行多Token预测和统一多目标排序模块，并引入任务感知Token增强生成与排序的一致性，在电商推荐中取得提升。

quick_skim.title: Seeing and Reflecting: Multimodal Memory-Enhanced Agent Collaboration for Recommendation
quick_skim.abstract: Large language model (LLM)-based agentic recommender systems show promise in modeling user preferences through natural-language reasoning, yet they remain limited by text-centric inputs and coarse-grained memory updates, making agents prone to missing visual evidence, semantic noise, and preference drift. To address these limitations, we propose MMEACR, a Multimodal Memory-Enhanced Agent Collaboration framework for recommendation. MMEACR introduces a dual-track memory architecture that separates interpretable agent reasoning from fine-grained multimodal matching. In the reasoning track, collaborative User and Item Memory Agents maintain persistent multimodal memories and update them through an attribute-guided reinforcement-and-reflection mechanism. In the matching track, a decoupled multi-modal embedding memory is built from raw interaction narratives and item images to preserve detailed cross-modal signals beyond structured memory updates. The two tracks are integrated through weighted Reciprocal Rank Fusion to produce robust and interpretable rankings. Experiments on three real-world domains show that MMEACR achieves strong overall performance against competitive LLM-based and agent-based baselines, with notable gains in visually grounded recommendation scenarios.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: multimodal memory-enhanced agent for user preference modeling
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多模态记忆增强代理用于用户偏好建模
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 多模态记忆增强代理用于用户偏好建模
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 多模态记忆增强代理用于用户偏好建模
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: MMEACR uses dual-track memory with multimodal matching to improve user preference modeling in LLM-based recommenders.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 基于LLM的推荐智能体受限于纯文本输入和粗粒度记忆更新。本文提出多模态记忆增强协作框架MMEACR，采用双轨记忆架构分离推理与多模态匹配，通过属性引导的强化反思机制持久化用户和商品记忆，有效缓解视觉缺失和偏好漂移问题，提升推荐准确性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 基于LLM的推荐智能体受限于纯文本输入和粗粒度记忆更新。本文提出多模态记忆增强协作框架MMEACR，采用双轨记忆架构分离推理与多模态匹配，通过属性引导的强化反思机制持久化用户和商品记忆，有效缓解视觉缺失和偏好漂移问题，提升推荐准确性。

quick_skim.title: Every Sample Counts: Supervised Fine-Tuning of Language Models with Pointwise Constraints
quick_skim.abstract: Fine-tuning language models often requires enforcing constraints on individual inputs without compromising downstream performance. Existing constrained alignment methods impose constraints on average, which can induce undesirable disparities across inputs or users. We propose a novel alignment framework that addresses this gap by enforcing per-sample constraints while still minimizing an average loss. To mitigate the impact of overly restrictive constraints and outliers, we introduce a learned, sample-dependent relaxation that minimally adjusts the constraints, trading off a user-defined relaxation cost with the training objective. To address practical duality and optimization challenges, we develop an augmented Lagrangian approach tailored to this formulation. We demonstrate the flexibility of the framework by instantiating it under distinct small language-model fine-tuning tasks and constraints: safety in instruction following, preferences in function calling and length in re-ranking. Across these settings, our approach reduces tail constraint violations while largely preserving the model's performance.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Fine-tuning LLMs with per-sample constraints to address user disparities
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过逐样本约束微调LLM以解决用户差异
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过逐样本约束微调LLM以解决用户差异
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过逐样本约束微调LLM以解决用户差异
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper proposes a fine-tuning method that enforces per-sample constraints using an augmented Lagrangian approach, allowing personalized adaptation of LLMs by controlling disparities across users.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有大语言模型对齐方法常基于平均约束，导致不同输入或用户间出现性能差异。本文提出逐样本约束对齐框架，通过增广拉格朗日方法在最小化平均损失的同时强制执行每个样本的约束，并引入样本依赖的松弛机制处理过紧约束。实验表明该方法能有效减少用户间差异，为大模型个性化微调提供了新思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有大语言模型对齐方法常基于平均约束，导致不同输入或用户间出现性能差异。本文提出逐样本约束对齐框架，通过增广拉格朗日方法在最小化平均损失的同时强制执行每个样本的约束，并引入样本依赖的松弛机制处理过紧约束。实验表明该方法能有效减少用户间差异，为大模型个性化微调提供了新思路。
